_note: Source of truth for sprint metrics. Tables in RESULTS.md are generated from this data. After a sprint: (1) python3 tools/import_sprint.py --from <import-json> to import, (2) python3 tools/generate_tables.py <command> to regenerate tables, (3) paste into RESULTS.md.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: All 3 phases produced useful output. No phase was ceremonial.

hypotheses.name: 5-skill set is right for all projects
hypotheses.evidence: All 5 contributed. UX least relevant for CLI.

hypotheses.name: Consensus agent works for planning
hypotheses.evidence: No contradictions between skill perspectives.

hypotheses.name: Wave parallelism helps
hypotheses.evidence: ~40% speedup from parallel execution.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gates passed first try. No real issues to catch on a small milestone.

hypotheses.name: 30-40% context budget is right
hypotheses.evidence: Context did not become an issue. Observe again.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 3/5 compliant, 1 N/A, 1 non-compliant.

hypotheses.name: Consensus vs architect-only
hypotheses.evidence: Consensus wins for security phases. Consensus: 23 scenarios + sanitizer registry separation. Architect-only: 10 scenarios, no separation.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: All 4 gates passed first try. Coverage + lint gates enforce thresholds but did not catch a regression this sprint.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 (up from 3/5). TDD ordering still partial.

hypotheses.name: Coverage gate catches regressions
hypotheses.evidence: Coverage rose 4.10pp; gate enforces 65% floor.

hypotheses.name: Lint gate catches dead code
hypotheses.evidence: 7 pre-existing errors cleaned; no new ones introduced.

hypotheses.name: Haiku is viable for mechanical tasks
hypotheses.evidence: No haiku used. Architect didn't classify any tasks as mechanical.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 3rd consecutive sprint. THINK->EXECUTE->SHIP clean.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gates passed first try again (3rd in a row). Lint gate confirmed 0 new errors. No gate actually blocked anything.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 + 1 partial. PM Gherkin: YES. Architect cheapest model: YES (first haiku tasks). TDD: YES. Security hash validation: YES. Orchestrator read full impl (partial).

hypotheses.name: Coverage gate catches regressions
hypotheses.evidence: T4 (config): 5 tool uses, 13s, clean. T3 (CLI wiring): 25 tool uses, 61s, clean but higher cost for multi-file work.

hypotheses.name: Lint gate catches dead code
hypotheses.evidence: 15 -> 15, zero new errors introduced.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 4th consecutive sprint. Clean THINK->EXECUTE->SHIP.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gate 2 (lint) caught unused `relative` import from T1 subagent. 16 errors > 15 max. Fixed in 1 cycle. First time a gate actually blocked code across all Uluka sprints.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 5/5. PM Gherkin: YES. Architect cheapest model: YES (haiku for CLI wiring). ProdEng tests first: YES. Security input validation: YES. UX help text: YES. First perfect compliance score.

hypotheses.name: Coverage gate catches regressions
hypotheses.evidence: 70.22% > 65% floor. Steady upward trend.

hypotheses.name: Lint gate catches dead code
hypotheses.evidence: Gate 2 caught the unused import. First real enforcement action on Uluka.

sprints.label: DS S0
sprints.phase: M0 MVP: Terminal-based P2P encrypted messaging over Tor

metrics.gates_first_pass_note: clippy caught 3 issues, passed on second run

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Think produced coherent plan, Execute built 3,268 LOC in 7 waves, Ship gates all passed.

hypotheses.name: 5-skill set is right for all projects
hypotheses.evidence: All 5 contributed: PM (24 Gherkin scenarios), UX (CLI design), Architect (crate structure), PE (TDD/gates), Security (zeroization, Noise limits).

hypotheses.name: Wave parallelism helps
hypotheses.evidence: 4 of 7 waves used parallelism (2-3 agents). No merge conflicts. Module-per-file Rust structure maps naturally to independent waves.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Clippy caught manual_div_ceil, async_fn_in_trait. Security audit caught .expect() on network data and Noise message size exceeded.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5. PM Gherkin: YES. Architect cheapest model: YES (haiku for scaffold/CLI). TDD: PARTIAL. Zeroizing: YES. No disk writes: YES.

hypotheses.name: Flowstate works for greenfield projects
hypotheses.evidence: Empty repo to 3,268 LOC, 57 tests, all gates passing.

hypotheses.name: Skills generalize across languages
hypotheses.evidence: Core patterns transferred. Two Rust-specific gaps: async trait dyn-compatibility, Noise message size limits.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 2nd consecutive sprint. Clean execution.

hypotheses.name: Wave parallelism helps
hypotheses.evidence: Wave 4 ran 3 parallel agents (olm tests, integration test, security audit) on independent files. No conflicts.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gates passed first try, but Wave 4.3 security audit (pre-gate) caught .unwrap() on transport, bare Vec<u8> for plaintext, missing size guard. Audit-before-gates is effective.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 5/5 (1 partial). PM Gherkin: YES. Architect async traits: YES. TDD: PARTIAL (same structural issue as S0). Zeroizing: YES. Noise cap: YES.

hypotheses.name: Skills generalize across languages
hypotheses.evidence: Sprint 0's 3 change proposals (async trait, Noise limit, TDD clarification) prevented the same errors in Sprint 1. No new language-specific gaps.

sprints.label: DS S2
sprints.phase: M2 Phase 1: Relay Protocol and Message Store

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: M2 Phase 1 completed in 3 waves + gates. Plan -> execute -> ship pipeline continues to work.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Clippy caught `or_insert_with` -> `or_default` on first gate run. Fixed in 1 cycle.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 audited instructions complied (3 YES, 1 N/A-YES, 1 NO). The per-wave commit instruction was violated -- all waves committed together.

hypotheses.name: Self-contained modules are cheaper
hypotheses.evidence: Sprint 2 used 50% fewer tokens than Sprint 1. The relay has no dependencies on the crypto stack -- agents did not need to understand Noise/Olm to build it. Module independence directly reduces context cost.

metrics.subagent_note: 1S+2H, per retro (2 spawned via Task tool)

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 5th consecutive sprint. Combined THINK+EXECUTE merged for 2nd time. Hardening phase (tests + docs) completed in 3 tasks across 2 waves.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: All gates passed first attempt this sprint. Sprint 3's gate failure remains the canonical confirmation. Gate 2 staying at 15 confirms the "lint check in subagent prompts" fix from Sprint 3 is working.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4.5/5 complied. The partial (UX error messages) is an existing gap from Sprint 3, not a Sprint 4 regression. Core skills (architect, ProdEng, security, PM) all fully followed.

hypotheses.name: Coverage gate catches regressions
hypotheses.evidence: T2 (README, 156 lines, 43s) and T3 (version + CHANGELOG, 17s) both completed cleanly. Largest haiku task yet (T2) had zero fix cycles.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 6th consecutive sprint. Tech debt sprint (no features) still fits the 3-phase model cleanly. THINK was brief (plan inline), EXECUTE was 2 waves, SHIP is this retro.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gate 2 target changed from "max 15" to "0 errors". If Gate 2 had been 0 from the start, the 15 lint errors would have been caught in Sprint 0. The relaxed threshold allowed debt accumulation.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/4 applicable instructions complied. PM instruction N/A for tech debt (no user stories). All other skills followed with evidence.

sprints.label: DS S3
sprints.phase: M2 Phase 2: Relay Integration and Two-Peer Verification

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 7th consecutive sprint. Feature sprint with 72 new tests fits cleanly.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gates passed first attempt. 0 lint errors maintained. Coverage +2.85pp.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 2/3 applicable fully complied, 1 partial (PM Gherkin). Acceptance criteria written but not in strict Given/When/Then format.

sprints.label: DS S4
sprints.phase: M3 Phase 1: Gossip Protocol and Routing

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 12m session, 1357 LOC, all gates first pass

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: 4 subagents, wave-based execution, zero gate failures

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 2 complied, 1 N/A, 2 NO (1 carry-forward threat model, 1 infeasible crypto constraint)

sprints.label: Uluka S7
sprints.phase: Phase 21: Crypto Library Knowledge Base (NO-FLOWSTATE BASELINE)

metrics.gates_first_pass_note: manual: tsc + vitest + lint all pass

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Baseline completed without phases. 7.7x faster than Flowstate avg. Structure adds overhead on well-scoped milestones.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: No skills loaded. Blind quality review: 19/25 (76%) vs self-assessed 100% on Flowstate sprints. Input validation weakest (3/5).

metrics.gates_first_pass_note: manual: cargo build + clippy + test all pass

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Baseline completed without phases. 4.4x faster than DS Flowstate avg. Structure adds overhead on well-scoped milestones.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: No skills loaded. Blind quality review: 22/25 (88%) vs self-assessed 100% on Flowstate sprints. Dead code found (process_message_resilient).

sprints.label: DS S6
sprints.phase: M4 Phase 1 + M5 Phase 1: Tor spike + Matrix outbound bridge

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Sprint 6 covered two milestones (M4+M5) in a single 3-phase sprint. Think->Execute->Ship workflow held despite combined scope.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: All 4 gates passed on first attempt. 212 tests, 0 clippy warnings. Gates validated correctness of 2,652 new LOC across 2 new modules.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: Security auditor skill followed: Zeroizing wrappers maintained, no unwrap on network data, timeouts present. Architect skill followed: trait-based transport abstraction, async patterns. PM skill: Gherkin acceptance criteria written. However, no separate security review pass was run as recommended by security-auditor deployment model.

metrics.subagent_note: 5 audit agents + 2 fix agents. Metrics include blended M3.5 unblock agent work from same session.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Security audit/hardening sprint completed in 3-phase workflow. Think phase produced acceptance criteria and wave plan. Execute phase used 5 parallel opus audit agents + serial fix waves. All gates passed first try.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gates verified 0 regressions from security fixes (390 unit tests, 119 E2E tests pass). npm run check confirmed no new TypeScript errors. Build clean. The security audit itself was the 'gate' — found 2 critical, 9 high vulnerabilities.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: H7 audit: (1) No console.* in client src/ — PASS (only a comment reference found). (2) Coverage >= 75% — PASS (390 tests). (3) Crypto waves serial — PASS (no crypto files modified, audit-only). (4) Acceptance criteria had happy+failure scenarios — PASS. (5) No new UI added — N/A (hardening sprint).

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: UX/accessibility sprint completed in 3-phase workflow. Think (consensus) → Execute (4 waves with parallel agents) → Ship. Gates passed first try. Different project type (UI/UX) vs Sprint 1 (security audit).

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: E2E tests caught coach marks overlay blocking all room interactions (61 failures). Fixed with custom Playwright fixtures. svelte-check caught orphaned CSS selector (.theme-toggle-btn). Unit tests verified ARIA attributes.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: See retrospective for 5-instruction audit. All 5 compliant: TDD (tests written per module), no plaintext, model matching (haiku for mechanical, sonnet for logic), keyboard navigation (focus-visible), no GitHub milestone (N/A for UX sprint).

sprints.label: Sprint 8: Crypto Library Knowledge Base + Evidence Quality
sprints.phase: Phase 21: Crypto Library Knowledge Base + Evidence Quality

metrics.subagent_note: 1 Explore (codebase research) + 1 Haiku (subagent from prior session)
metrics.gates_first_pass_note: 3 regressions from refactor commit: confidence threshold, .find() vs .some() type error, unused import

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 8th consecutive sprint. Multi-session sprint with regression fix completed cleanly.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Gates caught 3 regressions from refactor commit: wrong threshold in confidence-booster, type error in verification-engine, unused import in verify.ts. All would have been silent bugs without gates.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 H7 instructions complied. PM Gherkin not applied (sprint was a continuation/fix session, not fresh planning). All other skills followed.
